page: Microsoft Azure FX8-2mds_v2 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the FXmdsv2 family, virtualized via Microsoft Hyper-V. It features an Intel Xeon 8573C processor operating at 4.2 GHz with 1 physical core and 2 dedicated vCPUs, supporting 2 threads per core. The system is equipped with a 260 MB L3 cache, 168.0 GB of system memory yielding 84.0 GB per core, and 472 GB of local storage. No GPUs are included in this configuration.

Benchmark data reveals a strong contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. The server achieves top-tier single-core performance in Geekbench with a score of 2344.0 and single-threaded gzip compression at 34.85 GB/sec. However, due to its low core count of 1 physical core, multi-core benchmarks are limited, with stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and Geekbench multi-core scores falling into the bottom 10% and bottom 25% respectively. Memory bandwidth is also relatively low, with large-block read-only bandwidth at 21.09 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 14.48 GB/sec.

This server offers a highly specialized resource profile, pairing a massive memory-to-core ratio of 84 GB per core with high single-core CPU speeds. This configuration is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring substantial memory footprints but minimal parallel processing, avoiding the expense of licensing or provisioning excess CPU cores. However, the low multi-core capacity and weak memory bandwidth present clear tradeoffs for highly parallelized tasks. It is best suited for memory-intensive, single-threaded operations, lightweight database tasks, or caching layers that do not require high multi-core throughput.
description: Microsoft Azure FX8-2mds_v2 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server from the FXmdsv2 family, running on Microsoft Hyper-V. It features a single physical Intel Xeon 8573C core providing 2 dedicated vCPUs at 4.2 GHz, paired with 168.0 GB of RAM and 472 GB of local storage. Benchmarks show top-tier single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2344.0 and single-threaded gzip compression of 34.85 GB/sec. However, multi-core performance is weak due to the single-core hardware limitation, and memory bandwidth is relatively low. This server is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-intensive workloads that do not require high parallel processing, such as specialized memory-heavy single-threaded applications, small database operations, or caching.
og_description: Microsoft Azure FX8-2mds_v2 is a memory-optimized server featuring 2 vCPUs on an Intel Xeon 8573C processor, 168 GB of RAM, and 472 GB of local storage, delivering strong single-core performance.

tagline: A memory-intensive server combining high single-core clock speeds with an exceptional memory-to-core ratio and local storage.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 2 dedicated vCPUs at 4.2 GHz
168.0 GB of RAM providing an extreme 84.0 GB per core ratio
472 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Top-tier single-core performance in Geekbench and gzip compression
Suited for memory-intensive, single-threaded workloads and caching
